data.title: Stock Market Today Review: SPY, QQQ Lose Steam on Labor Market Woes as Trump Calls for Iran's 'Unconditional Surrender'
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed in negative territory on Friday after the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) published February's jobs report as the U.S.-Iran war entered its seventh day.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

News That Moved the Stock Market Today

  • Nonfarm payrolls fell by 92,000 in February, well below the consensus estimate of 55,000 additions.
  • The BLS also revised January and December's nonfarm payrolls lower by a combined 69,000 jobs.
  • The unemployment rate rose to 4.4% from 4.3% in January.
  • President Trump urged Iran to surrender and turned down the idea of signing a deal with the country. "There will be no deal with Iran except UNCONDITIONAL SURRENDER!" he said in a Truth Social post.
  • BlackRock (BLK) was one of the worst performers in the S&P 500 (SPX) after the firm limited withdrawals in one of its flagship private credit funds.
  • Oracle (ORCL) closed lower following a report that it had scrapped plans to expand an AI data center in collaboration with OpenAI in Texas.
  • AMD (AMD) fell after TipRanks five-star analyst Stacy Rasgon warned that tighter U.S. export restrictions could slow global AI adoption.
  • Nvidia (NVDA), alongside the iShares Semiconductor ETF (SOXX), also posted losses on the day.

Today's Best-Performing Sector 

Consumer staples were the best-performing sector on Friday as investors took on a risk-off approach amid declines in the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX). Consumer staples stocks are seen as defensive because demand for essential goods like food, beverages, and household products tends to remain stable even during economic slowdowns. In addition, companies in the industry are often able to pass down costs to consumers because their products are viewed as indispensable instead of discretionary.

Several stocks led the consumer staples sector's gains, including:

  • Costco (COST)
  • Bunge (BG)
  • Kroger (KR)
  • Clorox (CLX)
  • Kimberly-Clark (KMB)

Today's Worst-Performing Sector 

That brings us to the consumer discretionary sector, which was the worst-performing group. With a rising unemployment rate and higher gas prices, spending on travel, dining out, and entertainment could take a toll as consumers choose to stay in and conserve money. In addition, the ongoing U.S.-Iran war could dampen consumer sentiment and weigh on retailers and other discretionary businesses.

Notable consumer discretionary stocks trading lower include:

  • Carnival (CCL)
  • Chipotle (CMG)
  • Ralph Lauren (RL)
  • MGM Resorts (MGM)
  • Norwegian Cruise Line (NCLH)
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Cathie Wood Ends a Rocky Week with These Trades – HOOD, JOBY, ROKU, DKNG, JD
data.description:
Wall Street wrapped up a turbulent week as escalating U.S.–Iran tensions and the latest U.S. jobs report kept investors on edge. As volatility gripped Wall Street, Cathie Wood's ARK Invest ETFs (exchange-traded funds) made several portfolio changes on Friday, March 6, according to the firm's daily trade disclosures. The well-known investor executed multiple buy and sell trades across technology, fintech, and aviation stocks.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Here's a quick look at ARK's latest moves.

What Did Cathie Wood Buy on March 6?

Joby Aviation (JOBY) – Wood made one of the day's largest purchases in the electric air-taxi company, buying 289,417 shares worth about $2.78 million. The move shows ARK's continued focus on the emerging electric aviation sector. The company recently reported its earnings results, posting a Q4 2025 loss of $0.14 per share, narrower than the $0.23 loss a year earlier. Following the results, Cathie Wood has been accumulating Joby shares.

Robinhood Markets (HOOD) – ARK also increased its stake in the fintech platform, purchasing 19,206 shares valued at roughly $1.55 million. The firm capitalized on the stock's roughly 4% drop on Friday.

JD Logistics (HK:2618) – The firm bought 1,129,547 shares of the logistics company for about $1.48 million, highlighting interest in tech-driven logistics businesses. The move came as the stock jumped roughly 22% on Friday, making it one of the day's notable gainers.

DraftKings (DKNG) – ARK added 10,600 shares of the sports-betting company, valued at around $269,876.

Here's What Wood Sold

Roku (ROKU) – The largest trade of the day was the sale of 32,304 shares worth about $3.17 million across several ARK funds. The move continues ARK's recent trend of trimming its Roku position.

Iridium Communications (IRDM) – ARK sold 86,890 shares of the satellite communications company for roughly $2.08 million.

10x Genomics (TXG) – Wood also reduced exposure to the genomics firm, selling 75,007 shares valued at about $1.62 million.

How Wall Street Views These Stocks

Let's see how these stocks perform using the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool.

Currently, Robinhood, DraftKings, and Roku carry a Strong Buy rating from analysts, while JD Logistics and 10x Genomics are rated Moderate Buy. Among them, Robinhood offers the highest upside potential at about 65% over the next 12 months.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: 'This Could Change the AWS Story,' Says BofA about Amazon Stock
data.description:
Amazon (AMZN) could benefit from rapid growth at artificial-intelligence startup Anthropic, according to top BofA Securities analyst Justin Post, who reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $275 price target. The analyst said Anthropic's accelerating adoption may signal stronger demand for Amazon Web Services (AWS), since the AI company relies heavily on Amazon's cloud infrastructure to train and run its models.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Recently, Bloomberg reported that Anthropic's annualized revenue run rate has surpassed $19 billion, up about $17 billion year-over-year and roughly $10 billion higher than at the end of 2025. The sharp increase suggests that demand for Anthropic's AI tools is rising quickly.

Anthropic Growth Could Boost AWS Sentiment

Post, a five-star analyst, said Anthropic's rapid increase in revenue appears to be driven by strong adoption of its Claude AI models and the developer tool Claude Code. The launch of Opus 4.6, the company's most advanced model so far, also helped accelerate adoption by improving the system's ability to handle complex coding tasks and operate across large codebases.

Anthropic is also seeing rising consumer adoption. The company said free active users have grown more than 60%, while daily signups have quadrupled since January.

Because Anthropic runs much of its AI infrastructure on AWS, stronger usage of these tools could translate directly into higher cloud spending on Amazon's platform.

What It Means for Amazon's AWS Business

The analyst estimates that Anthropic alone could contribute around $1 billion in quarter-over-quarter revenue growth for AWS as demand for AI computing increases.

Importantly, Post noted that Anthropic's success does not appear to be slowing other AI companies. OpenAI recently raised its medium-term revenue outlook, suggesting that demand across the AI ecosystem remains strong.

For now, Post believes the rapid expansion of AI developers and models could continue to drive cloud infrastructure demand, which may help support Amazon's long-term AWS growth story.

Is Amazon a Buy, Sell, or Hold? 

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on AMZN stock based on 40 Buys and three Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average AMZN price target of $282.21 per share implies 30.16% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Apple Stock (AAPL) Wins a New 'Street-High' Price Target
data.description:
Apple (AAPL), the tech giant behind the iPhone and Mac, received a new Street-high price target of $350 from top Wedbush analyst Daniel Ives, implying about 34% upside from current levels. He reiterated his Outperform rating on the stock. The five-star analyst believes Apple's latest Mac lineup powered by new AI chips could help drive stronger demand and bring more users into the company's ecosystem.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

It is worth noting that Ives ranks 728 out of more than 12,000 analysts tracked by TipRanks. He has a success rate of 52%, with an average return per rating of 11.90% over a one-year timeframe.

Why Wedbush Sees More Upside Now

Apple recently introduced several new laptops, including updated MacBook Air and MacBook Pro models powered by its new M5 chips, which are designed to improve performance for AI tasks while offering faster memory and longer battery life.

The company also unveiled a new MacBook Neo, a lower-priced 13-inch laptop starting at $599 aimed at more cost-focused buyers. According to Wedbush, the updated lineup is designed to support on-device AI workloads while also expanding Apple's reach across different price points.

Importantly, Ives noted that nearly half of Mac buyers are still new to the platform. That trend suggests Apple is continuing to attract first-time users rather than relying only on upgrades from existing customers.

Wedbush believes the broader lineup, from high-end AI laptops to a lower-priced entry model, could help expand Apple's user base and provide "incremental upside" to Mac sales going forward.

Is Apple a Buy or Sell Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on AAPL stock based on 15 Buys, nine Holds, and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average AAPL price target of $306.12 per share implies 17.6% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Rate Cut Odds Strengthen as Fed Officials Take Note of Sluggish Labor Market
data.description:
February's jobs report took many by surprise, including members of the Fed. In February, nonfarm payrolls dropped by 92,000, while the unemployment rate rose to 4.4% from 4.3%. In addition, January's and December's nonfarm payrolls were revised lower by 4,000 and 65,000, respectively.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"The hopes that the labor market was steadying, maybe that was too much, and we really have to keep our eye on the labor market," Federal Reserve Bank of San Francisco President Mary Daly told CNBC on Friday.

Rate Cut Odds Rise as Hammack, Collins Back Steady Rates

Furthermore, Federal Reserve Bank of Cleveland President Beth Hammack said that she sees "two-sided risks to rates," referencing the central bank's dual mandate of maximizing employment and keeping inflation near the 2% level.

Still, Hammack, as well as Boston Fed President Susan Collins, voiced their support for keeping interest rates on hold. While the Fed is expected to leave rates unchanged this month, the odds of a 25-bps rate cut at the April 29 Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) jumped to 19.5% from 11.4% a day ago on the CME FedWatch tool.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: S&P 500 Rebalancing: Who's In, Who's Out?
data.description:
The S&P Dow Jones Indices rebalances its major stock indices every quarter, and the company announced four new additions this time. The S&P 500 Index (SPX) is set to add Vertiv (VRT), Lumentum (LITE), Coherent (COHR), and EchoStar (SATS). These will join the SPX before the market opens on March 23.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

They replace Match Group (MTCH), Molina Healthcare (MOH), Lamb Weston (LW), and Paycom Software (PAYC). In the past three months, Lamb Weston and Paycom have declined 22.7% and 15.8%, respectively.  

The S&P 500 additions reflect the index's goal of accurately capturing the current scenario of large-cap U.S. companies. Vertiv, Lumentum, and Coherent have all benefited from the global surge in AI infrastructure spending. Their rapid growth in data‑center power systems, optical components, and high-speed connectivity pushed them into large-cap territory.

Also, Lumentum, Coherent, and EchoStar were moved up from the S&P MidCap 400 after their market value grew too large for the mid-cap index. This type of "migration" is common when companies outgrow their previous category.

Recent S&P 100 Additions 

The newest additions to the S&P 100 are Micron Technology (MU), Lam Research (LRCX), Applied Materials (AMAT), and GE Vernova (GEV).

To make room for these additions, the following companies are being removed from the S&P 100 but will remain in the S&P 500. These include PayPal (PYPL), American International Group (AIG), MetLife (MET), and Target (TGT).

Which Stock Is a Better Buy?

Let's see how the S&P 500's newly added four stocks perform on the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool. Among all the stocks, SATS stock offers the highest upside potential of 16.34% over the next twelve months. Meanwhile, analysts remain more bullish on Vertiv's long-term outlook.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: IBM Uses Quantum-Supercomputer Combo to Model Newly Discovered Half-Möbius Molecule
data.description:
Scientists from tech firm IBM (IBM) and several universities have created a completely new type of molecule that has never been observed before. The discovery, published in the journal Science, involves what researchers describe as a "half-Möbius" molecule. For context, a Möbius strip is a famous geometric shape that twists once and has only one continuous side. Interestingly, the electrons in this molecule move along a similar twisting path, which gives the structure unusual properties that scientists believe could eventually help develop new materials or advanced electronic technologies.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

To build the molecule, researchers used extremely precise tools that allow them to position atoms one at a time. The resulting molecule contains 13 carbon atoms and two chlorine atoms arranged in a ring. However, unlike typical molecular rings, the electrons traveling around this structure follow a twisting path that rotates about 90 degrees during each loop. Because of this, electrons must travel four full circuits around the ring before returning to their original orientation, which is what gives the molecule its unique half-Möbius behavior.

Importantly, though, understanding how this unusual molecule works required more than standard computer simulations. As a result, the researchers combined quantum computing with traditional supercomputers to model how the electrons behave inside the structure. These simulations confirmed that the molecule can exist in different configurations, such as left-twisted, right-twisted, or untwisted forms. Ultimately, the discovery shows that scientists may eventually be able to design entirely new types of molecules on purpose, while also demonstrating how quantum computers can help solve scientific problems that are extremely difficult for traditional computers to analyze.

Is IBM a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on IBM stock based on 12 Buys, six Holds, and zero Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average IBM price target of $334.18 per share implies 30.4% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri
